title: MANGA GOS2 Angelic Days what a let down
display_title: MANGA GOS2 Angelic Days what a let down
wikitext:
[]: [GOS2] Angelic Days... what a let down.             Quote Post by Kyle » Thu Apr 19, 2007 9:15 pm I bought vol.4 of angelic days the other day much to my regret.I won't spoil anything(Not that there's anything "OMG" to spoil). I was like "That's it?He's(The author) gonne do it like that?"My biggest complaint was the flow of the story,way too fast if you ask me.Second gripe is the lack of dialog boxes.I coudn't tell who was saying what most of the time.The dialog itself was another problem.I could get by with the art style but it didn't draw(no pun intended)me in like most other manga artists can do with work.And there was the sense of being lost.I mean how many things were left unsettled?(Gendo & shinjis relationship to name one) I for one no longer care for this book.I will refuse to buy it anymore. I was so phyched up for it but it all went down hill for me after vol.2.But I gave it another chance and was met with CRAP. If you LOVE eva to no end by all means buy this book.But if your like me and just wanted a new good story with familier elements look elswhere.

[]:             Quote Post by Tokpile Quohog » Thu Apr 19, 2007 9:18 pm Same here, I felt kinda pissed of that the author just ended it as soon as it started.

[]: angelic 4             Quote Post by gydromon » Thu Apr 19, 2007 11:24 pm that's not what i want to hear-

[]:             Quote Post by MSimm62385 » Fri Apr 20, 2007 12:00 am There are no spoilers really. The cover art says it all. Being a bit of an A/S, I was left kinda happy, but I agree that its execution wasn't that great. <SPOILERS TAG =O> If you're going to separate all the pilots to separate countries, why would you skip over the entire thing and bring them back for a mushy reunion in the very next chapter? It didn't need to take something that drastic to get Asuka and Shinji to open up to each other, did it? </SPOILERS TAG =O> I am however, intrigued on how this manga will cover the next story arc with young Gendo and Yui.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Fri Apr 20, 2007 7:50 am So, are the next two volumes short side stories, or one continuous side story? I can't really tell from my Japanese versions. I don't know moon-speak.

[]:             Quote Post by TriLink » Fri Apr 20, 2007 11:14 am The Eva Monkey wrote: So, are the next two volumes short side stories, or one continuous side story? I can't really tell from my Japanese versions. I don't know moon-speak.

[]:             Quote Post by Shawn Dudley » Fri Apr 20, 2007 12:31 pm Angelic Days doesn't really make sense unless you've actually played the GOFS 2 game. If you're unfamiliar with the game (and there's some panels here on evamonkey that have gone over this earlier), it's basically a dating sim that takes place in the "Alternative" world of Episode 26 (you know, the one with "hyper" Rei). SPOILER For the most part, the situations in the manga (including the "Tunnel of Love" and Rei and Shinji's trip to Shin-Yokahama) are the same as in the game. There are a few differences - the manga covers the other characters like Toji/Hikari and even Gendo/Yui, and the manga has the whole Kaworu backstory bit that doesn't exist in the game, but all of the Shinji/Asuka/Rei-type situations are straight out of the game. The part where all of the pilots are assigned to positions around the world is also straight out of the game.

[]:             Quote Post by Trigger's Elysium » Fri Apr 20, 2007 3:24 pm Ah, so it ends Shinji/Asuka? I predicted so. *crosses off list of things to buy*

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Fri Apr 20, 2007 9:04 pm 1) The cover's a dead giveaway it's Shinji/Asuka, hell you could even see the covers for Volumes 1-6 on Gainax's site. 2) The story doesn't END at Volume 4. 5 is a prologue-type story with Gendo, Kaworu and Yui in middle school while Vol.6 has short stories of Kaji/Misato, Hikari/Toji, Rei/Ritsuko ( not romantically), and finally Shinji/Asuka which takes place sometime after the events of Vol.4 (I can't remember if it's the same for the other stories.) I'm assuming Vol.5 explains why Kaworu disappeared (in terms of Adam disappearing in Vol.4 at the end of the battle, the GoS2 game didn't explain that either :roll:) since it's not explained in Vol 4 or when we see him again in Vol.6. Also: what Shawn Dudley said.

[]:             Quote Post by Trigger's Elysium » Fri Apr 20, 2007 10:21 pm 1. That was pretty obvious anyway, as that's how it goes for trashy harem series 2. I probably wouldn't be able to appreciate it. For one, I still hate the Shinji/Asuka pairing, and number two, it'll probably have the same terrible writing as the rest of the harem did.

[]:             Quote Post by Shawn Dudley » Sat Apr 21, 2007 11:54 pm Actually, the best thing about the GFOS2 game is the "shipping" - if you're a fan of R/S, A/S, or even K/S, now's your chance to see all of those cute possibilities with the couple of your choice. There's even bits where they hint at all of the other coupling possibilities (and you all know who I mean). Reichu has done a FAQ on the game from some time back, and while her opinion was not high, it's probably worth the time to Torrent it and play it through at least once. I didn't mind the GFOS2 manga so much, but if you didn't play the game it's going to seem like so much triffle.

[]: angelic days 4             Quote Post by gydromon » Sun Apr 22, 2007 7:19 am i guess i'm in the minority so far- i didn't mind angelic days 4- if it were the last installment, then i'd probably feel a little differently- i'm not really into eva for the romantic relationships that may unfold between Shinji and any of the other children- i know it's there (especially in angelic days) and i do appreciate it- but, that aspect definitely takes a back seat to so many other elements- angelic days isn't my favorite manga by a long shot, but i do dig it and am kind of anxious to see where it's going next-

[]:             Quote Post by Jay911 » Sun Apr 22, 2007 12:45 pm I think I agree with the sentiment I saw posted somewhere months ago (and I forget where, or who said it), which was that Angelic Days is "an okay fanfic". Which is essentially what it is, anyway.

[]: angelic 4             Quote Post by gydromon » Sat Apr 28, 2007 5:36 pm Jay911 wrote: I think I agree with the sentiment I saw posted somewhere months ago (and I forget where, or who said it), which was that Angelic Days is "an okay fanfic". Which is essentially what it is, anyway.

[]:             Quote Post by Trigger's Elysium » Sat Apr 28, 2007 7:26 pm For me, a terrible harem fanfic with eye scarringly bad artwork that's both terribly written and misses the whole point of the AU.

[]:             Quote Post by Zkh » Sat Apr 28, 2007 7:43 pm I love angelic days and i buy two of every volume so i can lick one and read the other at the same time. Also, you got what was coming for buying a non-canon manga produced only to give gainax more money.

[]:             Quote Post by Trigger's Elysium » Sat Apr 28, 2007 7:44 pm I never bought it, thankfully. I looked at scanlations.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sat Apr 28, 2007 8:30 pm I enjoyed it (GoS2. I think in some ways the manga improved over the game), and hell I'd probably enjoy the Ikari Shinji Raising Project manga if I even get around to reading it, A/S or not, but then again, this is coming from someone who doesn't really care for Sadamoto's "retelling" of the original story. ;

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sat Apr 28, 2007 8:56 pm Every time you say "Angelic Days", Matt Greenfield and Tiffany Grant have sex.

[]:             Quote Post by TriLink » Sat Apr 28, 2007 8:59 pm Reichu wrote: Every time you say "Angelic Days", Matt Greenfield and Tiffany Grant have sex.
